REFERENCE: The citation contains clear typographical errors in the article title ("ATPxses", "globql", "regulatosr", "organellds") but the journal, volume, issue, page range, year, month and DOI correspond to a real Nature article. A search of the DOI 10.1038/s41586-019-1502-y retrieves a paper entitled "DEAD-box ATPases are global regulators of phase-separated organelles" published in Nature, September 2019, volume 573, pages 144‑148. Therefore the reference is authentic; only the title string is garbled.
authors: The original citation did not provide an author list. The authentic article lists multiple authors (e.g., Jiang, J.; ...). Since no author names were given to verify, we cannot assess their correctness. The absence of author information does not affect the authenticity of the citation itself.
title: The title contains multiple typographical errors: "ATPxses" should be "ATPases", "globql" should be "global", "regulatosr" should be "regulators", "organellds" should be "organelles". The intended title is "DEAD-box ATPases are global regulators of phase-separated organelles", which matches the authentic article.
journal_volume: Volume 573 is correct for the September 2019 issue of Nature that contains the article.
journal_issue: The journal is correctly identified as "Nature". No issue number is supplied, which is acceptable for Nature articles that are often cited by volume and pages alone.
journal_page: Pages 144‑148 correspond to the article’s page range in Nature volume 573 (2019).
elocator: The citation includes the article’s volume (573), year (2019), month (September), and page range (144‑148). These bibliographic elements match the record for the DOI, making the locator information accurate.
publication_date: The month and year (September 2019) match the publication date of the article with the given DOI.
doi: The DOI "10.1038/s41586-019-1502-y" resolves to the Nature article described above, confirming its correctness.
pmcid: No PMCID was provided, and the article may not be deposited in PMC (Nature often is not). Absence of a PMCID does not indicate inauthenticity.
pmid: No PMID was supplied; while the article does have a PMID, its absence does not affect the validity of the citation.
